messages.content: Build an event-sourcing command handler as an Effect from scratch. Load events for an aggregate, decide a new event from the command, then append it atomically.
How do I write an event-sourcing command handler in Effect v3 that validates the command, folds the event stream into state, and only appends when the invariant holds? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how an event-sourcing command handler composes with a typed error channel in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation.

Write an Effect v3 program that implements an event-sourcing command handler for a user-registration aggregate. Constraints: use Context.GenericTag for the store, Data.TaggedError for errors, no any, and bail with a tagged error when a duplicate email is detected.
Add idempotency-key support as a capability add-on to this v3 command handler. The handler should short-circuit with a `Duplicate` tagged error when the same key has already produced an event.

Refactor this handler to extract the decide function from the load/append scaffold so that the pure core can be unit-tested without the Context service: `const handle=(c)=>Effect.gen(function*(){const s=yield*Store; const evs=yield*s.load(); if(evs.length>5) return yield*Effect.fail(new Cap({})); const ev={_tag:'Did', n:evs.length+1}; yield*s.append(ev); return ev})`.
Build a batch scheduler from scratch that uses Effect.forEach with bounded concurrency and per-item retry via a composed Schedule.
How do I run a list of jobs in parallel with a fixed concurrency cap and retry each individual job up to N times in Effect v3? Show a minimal example.
Explain how Effect.forEach distinguishes concurrency modes, then show a typed batch scheduler implementation that processes 100 items, 10 at a time, each retried with jitter.

Add per-item timeout to this batch scheduler so each worker aborts if it takes longer than 500ms, merged into the existing retry policy: `const schedule=(ids:string[])=>Effect.forEach(ids, run, {concurrency:4})`.
Fix this v3 snippet — it runs jobs sequentially but the caller asked for concurrency=8: `Effect.forEach(ids, job)`.
Build a persistent message queue from scratch using Effect.Queue to buffer messages and a persist-to-store callback that flushes each dequeued message through an Effect.
What's the Effect v3 way to build a bounded queue whose consumer persists each message and whose producer back-pressures when full? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how a persistent queue differs from an in-memory one in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation that survives a worker crash.
Write an Effect v3 persistent queue pipeline. Constraints: bounded capacity=64, the store is injected via Context.GenericTag, consumer runs forever as a forked fiber, no any.

Add a dead-letter branch to this persistent queue consumer: messages that fail three persist attempts should be diverted to a `dlq` queue instead of crashing the worker: `Effect.forever(Effect.flatMap(Queue.take(main), save))`.
Build a rate-limited API client from scratch using an Effect.Semaphore to cap concurrent outbound requests at 3, wrapping a typed fetch effect.
How do I cap outbound API concurrency to N in Effect v3 so that bursty call sites queue politely instead of stampeding? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how a semaphore differs from a plain concurrency cap on Effect.all, then show a typed rate-limited client implementation that enforces 5 concurrent requests across N call sites.
Write an Effect v3 rate-limited HTTP client. Constraints: global cap of 4 in-flight requests via Semaphore, tagged error, strict types, expose a single `call(url)` function.
Add per-request timeout (2 seconds) to this semaphore-wrapped API caller so a stuck fetch can't starve the permit pool: `const call=(u:string)=>sem.withPermits(1)(rawCall(u))`.
Build a graceful-shutdown controller from scratch using Effect.Scope and finalizers. Acquiring the scope opens a DB connection; closing it must run the release even if the work fibre throws.
What's the Effect v3 way to tie a listener's teardown to process shutdown so the callback always unsubscribes, even on SIGINT? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how Scope guarantees finalizer execution on interruption in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation that opens two resources with an ordered release.
Write an Effect v3 graceful-shutdown controller. Constraints: tracks N open sockets, registers each close as a finalizer, supports cooperative interrupt via Effect.interrupt, no any.
Add a finalizer to this scoped program that flushes an in-memory log buffer on exit: `Effect.scoped(Effect.gen(function*(){ const db = yield* openDb; return db }))`.
Add an onRetry logging hook to this Effect retry so every attempt emits a structured log line: `Effect.retry(call, Schedule.jittered(Schedule.exponential(Duration.millis(100))))`.
Build an event-sourcing replay helper from scratch that folds a stream of events into an Effect-wrapped snapshot, short-circuiting on a corrupted-event tagged error.
